# Data Analytics Ecosystem — High-Level Needs

This document defines the high-level needs that serve as parent traces for
requirements throughout the data analytics ecosystem. Needs are derived
from the Concept of Operations and are tagged by domain:

* **N-EDDT** — Runtime system needs.
* **N-ECOL** — Organisational ecosystem lifecycle needs.

---

## 1. Runtime System Needs (EDDT)

### Functional Architecture

These needs govern what the runtime system does, mapped to the OODA loop
phases (Observe–Orient–Decide–Act).

* <a id="n-eddt-001"></a>**[N-EDDT-001][n-eddt-001] [Observability]:** The system must observe both the external
  environment and its own internal state. Observation is inherently
  asynchronous and may yield partial, overlapping, or stale coverage
  depending on the instrument and interfacing system.
* <a id="n-eddt-002"></a>**[N-EDDT-002][n-eddt-002] [Orientation & Modelling]:** Raw observations must be
  structured into a coherent, queryable model of reality through the
  EDDT and its tiered data lake. Given the asynchronous nature of
  observation ([N-EDDT-001][n-eddt-001]), the model accepts eventual consistency across
  representations and tiers, and must maintain traceable provenance to
  the originating data sources, sufficient to support historical
  reconstruction for verification, audit, debugging, and recovery.
* <a id="n-eddt-003"></a>**[N-EDDT-003][n-eddt-003] [Decision & Intelligence]:** The system must support the
  continuous maturation of analytical capability — from exploratory analysis
  through formalised algorithmics, calibration, and real-time signal
  generation. This progression is iterative: automated subsystems adapt and
  calibrate within operational cycles, while human-mediated processes advance
  capabilities across lifecycle iterations ([N-ECOL-001][n-ecol-001], [N-ECOL-002][n-ecol-002]).
* <a id="n-eddt-004"></a>**[N-EDDT-004][n-eddt-004] [Actuation & Response]:** The system must effect change —
  both automated (governors) and human-mediated (playbooks).

### Quality Attributes

These needs govern how well the runtime system performs its functions and
under what bounds.

* <a id="n-eddt-005"></a>**[N-EDDT-005][n-eddt-005] [Temporal Performance]:** The system must meet acceptable
  time-bounds across the data flow — from instrument ingestion through
  event processing to analytics consumption — distinguishing real-time
  (event subscription) from batch (columnar generation) latency envelopes.
* <a id="n-eddt-006"></a>**[N-EDDT-006][n-eddt-006] [Data Governance & Security]:** The system must enforce data
  sovereignty, PII handling, access control, and compliance constraints on
  ingested data across all tiers, storage representations, and event
  subjects.
* <a id="n-eddt-007"></a>**[N-EDDT-007][n-eddt-007] [Scalability & Data Velocity]:** The system must handle the
  data volumes and event rates characteristic of large-scale instrumented
  infrastructure observation — whether mining fleets, manufacturing lines,
  healthcare estates, or other instrumented domains — without degradation,
  governing capacity planning across instruments, event processing, and
  storage tiers.
* <a id="n-eddt-008"></a>**[N-EDDT-008][n-eddt-008] [Data Completeness & Fidelity]:** The system must provide
  measurable guarantees about completeness, freshness, and confidence in
  the data captured and preserved through each tier — supporting gap
  detection, staleness reporting, and quantitative trust assessment by
  downstream consumers. Measurability requires data provenance metadata —
  source instrument, ingestion timestamp, and data lineage — to be
  preserved alongside the data at each tier.

### Operational Lifecycle

These needs govern how the runtime system is operated, maintained, and
evolved over time.

* <a id="n-eddt-009"></a>**[N-EDDT-009][n-eddt-009] [Platform Operational Health]:** The system must continuously
  monitor and report on the health, throughput, and correctness of its own
  data processing pipelines, storage systems, and event delivery paths —
  independent of the customer network observation it performs.
* <a id="n-eddt-010"></a>**[N-EDDT-010][n-eddt-010] [System Evolvability]:** The system must accommodate change
  in domain models, notification payload schemas, notification addressing
  namespaces, message-routing contracts, storage schemas, and API
  interfaces — within stable architectural conventions — without
  requiring full-system downtime or data loss, supporting
  backward-compatible evolution, versioned interfaces, and schema migration
  across all tiers and points-of-view.
* <a id="n-eddt-011"></a>**[N-EDDT-011][n-eddt-011] [Data Retention & Lifecycle]:** The system must govern the
  retention, archival, and disposal of data across all tiers — balancing
  regulatory retention requirements ([N-EDDT-006][n-eddt-006]) against storage cost and
  operational sustainability.

---

## 2. Organisational Ecosystem Needs (ECOL)

### Development Process

These needs govern how the organisation develops and matures capabilities
(left side of the V-model through realisation).

* <a id="n-ecol-001"></a>**[N-ECOL-001][n-ecol-001] [Lifecycle Governance]:** The organisation must follow a
  structured lifecycle model (V-model, recursive iterations, accretion)
  governing how capabilities are developed and matured.
* <a id="n-ecol-002"></a>**[N-ECOL-002][n-ecol-002] [Human Participation]:** The lifecycle must define the roles,
  transitions, and contributions of human participants at each stage of the
  OODA loop and lifecycle.
* <a id="n-ecol-003"></a>**[N-ECOL-003][n-ecol-003] [Organisational Alignment]:** The lifecycle must provide a
  framework that aligns Engineering, Product, and Sales/Field expectations
  around capability maturity.
* <a id="n-ecol-004"></a>**[N-ECOL-004][n-ecol-004] [System Integration]:** The organisational ecosystem that
  evolves the automated systems must work within the technical constraints
  and capabilities of the EDDT, while informing the capabilities the EDDT
  needs to surface — covering both event subject views and tabular views
  that distil information originating from events.

### Design-Time Governance

* <a id="n-ecol-005"></a>**[N-ECOL-005][n-ecol-005] [Architectural Trade-off Management]:** The organisational
  lifecycle must include explicit mechanisms for recording, evaluating, and
  governing architectural compromises and their associated technical debt.

### Integration, Verification & Transition

These needs govern the right side of the V-model and the transition to
operations.

* <a id="n-ecol-008"></a>**[N-ECOL-008][n-ecol-008] [Verification & Validation]:** The organisation must define
  the quality gates and verification strategies required before any
  capability is permitted to accrete into the production runtime
  environment.
* <a id="n-ecol-009"></a>**[N-ECOL-009][n-ecol-009] [Capability Propagation]:** The organisational lifecycle
  must define how capabilities validated in individual deployments are
  generalised, extracted into the shared platform, and propagated to other
  deployments — ensuring that accretion is governed rather than accidental.

### Sustainment & Operations

These needs govern how the organisation operates, maintains, and eventually
retires deployed capabilities.

* <a id="n-ecol-006"></a>**[N-ECOL-006][n-ecol-006] [Sustainment & Disposal]:** The organisational ecosystem
  must define processes for continuously monitoring the health and accuracy
  of deployed capabilities (e.g. ML model drift, algorithm degradation as
  network technology evolves), performing routine maintenance, and safely
  deprecating obsolete components without breaking accreted dependencies.
* <a id="n-ecol-007"></a>**[N-ECOL-007][n-ecol-007] [Configuration & Baseline Management]:** The lifecycle must
  provide rigorous configuration management to ensure that accretion
  results in a stable, verifiable, and manageable shared platform baseline
  — encompassing source code, data models, deployment artefacts, and
  runtime parameters — across all deployments.
* <a id="n-ecol-010"></a>**[N-ECOL-010][n-ecol-010] [Operational Feedback]:** The organisational lifecycle must
  include structured mechanisms for capturing operational experience, field
  observations, and production behaviour data, and feeding these back into
  subsequent lifecycle iterations as inputs to the Business or Mission
  Analysis (BMA) and Stakeholder Needs (SNRD) processes.
* <a id="n-ecol-011"></a>**[N-ECOL-011][n-ecol-011] [External Interface Stewardship]:** The organisational
  ecosystem must define a process for tracking the evolution of relevant
  external interfaces — including standards-body specifications (e.g.
  ISO, IEC, IEEE, IETF, or industry-specific consortia such as ISA-95
  for manufacturing, HL7 / FHIR for healthcare, or domain-specific
  bodies for mining and other instrumented sectors), vendor APIs
  (instrument and OEM device vendors, infrastructure management APIs,
  endpoint device interfaces), and interoperating-platform contracts —
  and for translating their changes into inputs to capability evolution
  ([N-EDDT-010][n-eddt-010]), instrument conformance, and lifecycle iterations.
  This complements [N-ECOL-010][n-ecol-010] (operational feedback) by routing
  externally-driven change, not operations-driven change, into BMA / SNRD
  / SRD inputs.

---

## 3. INCOSE Technical Process Coverage

The following table maps each of the 14 INCOSE/ISO 15288 technical
processes to the needs that anchor them.

| Process                          | Abbrev | Anchoring Needs                                                                                        |
|:---------------------------------|:-------|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Business or Mission Analysis     | BMA    | [N-ECOL-001][n-ecol-001], [N-ECOL-010][n-ecol-010], [N-ECOL-011][n-ecol-011]                           |
| Stakeholder Needs & Requirements | SNRD   | [N-ECOL-002][n-ecol-002], [N-ECOL-003][n-ecol-003], [N-ECOL-010][n-ecol-010], [N-ECOL-011][n-ecol-011] |
| System Requirements Definition   | SRD    | [N-ECOL-001][n-ecol-001], [N-ECOL-004][n-ecol-004], [N-ECOL-011][n-ecol-011]                           |
| System Architecture Definition   | SAD    | [N-ECOL-004][n-ecol-004], [N-ECOL-005][n-ecol-005]                                                     |
| Design Definition                | DD     | [N-ECOL-004][n-ecol-004]                                                                               |
| System Analysis                  | SA     | [N-ECOL-005][n-ecol-005]                                                                               |
| Implementation                   | IMPL   | [N-ECOL-001][n-ecol-001]                                                                               |
| Integration                      | INT    | [N-ECOL-008][n-ecol-008]                                                                               |
| Verification                     | VER    | [N-ECOL-008][n-ecol-008]                                                                               |
| Transition                       | TRAN   | [N-ECOL-007][n-ecol-007], [N-ECOL-009][n-ecol-009]                                                     |
| Validation                       | VAL    | [N-ECOL-008][n-ecol-008]                                                                               |
| Operation                        | OPER   | [N-EDDT-009][n-eddt-009], [N-ECOL-006][n-ecol-006], [N-ECOL-010][n-ecol-010]                           |
| Maintenance                      | MAINT  | [N-EDDT-010][n-eddt-010], [N-EDDT-011][n-eddt-011], [N-ECOL-006][n-ecol-006], [N-ECOL-007][n-ecol-007] |
| Disposal                         | DISP   | [N-EDDT-011][n-eddt-011], [N-ECOL-006][n-ecol-006]                                                     |
